Head-to-Head in IPL:

This rivalry has been as close as it gets. Across 31 IPL meetings, Chennai Super Kings hold a slender edge with 16 victories, while Rajasthan Royals are right behind with 15 wins. The numbers reflect just how evenly matched these two sides have been over the years, setting the stage for yet another tight contest.



Match number three of the TATA IPL 2026 injects tradition and anticipation into the early phase of the tournament as Rajasthan Royals take on Chennai Super Kings in a contest steeped in legacy at the Barsapara Cricket Stadium. These were the two sides that contested the very first IPL final back in 2008, and as the league enters its 19th edition, the reunion arrives with new faces, new leadership strands and renewed urgency, even though it marks just the opening fixture of the season for both teams.

Both franchises come into this clash driven by correction and clarity after enduring a tough campaign last year, finishing in the bottom two. Squads have been reshaped, combinations refined and identities redefined. Rajasthan Royals look lighter, sharper and more flexible, while Chennai Super Kings arrive with their familiar blend of calm experience and fearless youth, determined to reassert their standard early.
One of the strongest subplots surrounds Sanju Samson, the former Royals captain, now lining up against his old franchise in yellow. Few know the opposition better, and that emotional edge could matter in tight moments. Equally intriguing is the youth battle between Mhatre and Suryavanshi, two exciting young prospects eager to seize their opportunity and announce themselves on a big stage.

Barsapara is ready for a vibrant evening, with energy in the stands and intent in the middle. Both teams understand the value of a strong first impression in a long tournament. History looms, hunger burns and belief waits to be validated.

Two champions, one fresh beginning. When the first ball is bowled, someone will set the tone early. Who draws first blood in IPL 2026?
MatchSummary.PostMatchCommentary:
Nandre Burger (Player of the Match):
"It feels really good to contribute early and help the team get off to a strong start. Bowling first was ideal for us and we had watched games here and prepared knowing conditions would assist the bowlers. Against Samson the plan was simple because every batter is a wicket and it comes down to using natural variation and forcing shots into areas we want. The dressing room feels the same as before with the same coaching staff and familiar environment. It is nice to see everyone smiling and starting the tournament on a positive note."
 

Nandre andar, dono openers bahar ???????? pic.twitter.com/IVs26vf4pJ

— Rajasthan Royals (@rajasthanroyals) March 30, 2026

Riyan Parag (Rajasthan Royals Captain):
"I felt the toss went our way and the execution that followed was excellent, especially after spending a few days practicing here before CSK arrived. Knowing it was a red soil pitch and that rain had added some moisture helped us plan better and everyone carried out their roles really well. Being the full time captain feels right because a lot of preparation and planning has gone into this team and I am grateful to the franchise for trusting me. The goal for us this season is very clear and that is to win the trophy after putting in a lot of work behind the scenes. We want to play smart cricket, adapt quickly to conditions and today was a good example of doing exactly that."
 

Ruturaj Gaikwad (Chennai Super Kings Captain):
"I did not feel overly frustrated because the conditions early on were difficult with the quicks bowling well and the spinners also getting help, so it was always going to be a tough surface to bat on. Our idea was to take the game deep and show calm batsmanship to push the score towards one hundred and fifty or one hundred and sixty, but in today’s game it is hard to know what a safe total really is. From here the focus is to move on quickly because we have another match in three days and everyone must stay in the right mental space. It helps that the group is young and eager, and on another day the same players can step up and deliver strong performances. With the impact player rule you have to stay positive while batting first and back your instincts because if the ball is there to be hit you must take that option."


Rajasthan Royals  combined sharp bowling figures with ruthless batting numbers to register a dominant win over Chennai Super Kings  at Guwahati, closing the chase in just 12.1 overs and underlining their control through every phase of the match.


Chennai Super Kings were restricted to 127 all out in 19.4 overs, a total shaped by early collapses that left them 41 for 4 inside the powerplay. Jofra Archer led the charge with figures of 4 overs for 19 runs and 2 wickets, while Nandre Burger matched him strike for strike, conceding only 26 across his spell. Ravindra Jadeja added control in the middle overs, picking up 2 for 18 as Chennai slipped to 74 for 7 by the 11th over. Jamie Overton top scored with 43 from 36 balls, accounting for more than a third of the total, but support remained limited.


The chase was effectively decided in the first six overs. Rajasthan raced to 74 without loss in the powerplay, driven by Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s explosive 52 off just 17 balls, struck at a staggering rate above 300. Yashasvi Jaiswal anchored the innings with an unbeaten 38 from 36 deliveries, ensuring stability as the target approached. The Royals crossed the line at 128 for 2, finishing with 47 balls remaining and sealing a win defined by numbers as much as authority.
